items.summary: Emergency hay deliveries to drought-stricken areas resume after thousands of dollars in donations from school students and charity groups.

items.title: Freshwater development triggers calls for protections of significant trees
items.summary: After several failed court challenges, a council has angered a community with its approval to remove a significant tree to make way for a duplex development, but experts say local governments need to start paying more attention to residents' concerns.

items.title: Four EzyMart Sydney stores among 17 retailers shut over illegal tobacco, vape sale claims
items.summary: The major Australian retail company will part ways with an independent operator after several Sydney stores were shut down for allegedly selling illegal tobacco and vapes.

items.title: Federal government to sue 3M Australia over firefighting foam that contained PFAS
items.summary: The Commonwealth is launching its largest-ever legal claim, suing the manufacturer of a firefighting foam containing PFAS chemicals that contaminated 28 Defence Force bases across Australia.

items.title: Buses 'unsafe and late' in Cairns as regional public transport 'struggles' to match population growth
items.summary: Amid a spate of assaults at bus stops in Far North Queensland, advocates say there's a need for change.

items.title: Hugh Marks denies 'threat' to force ABC news director out of role
items.summary: ABC managing director Hugh Marks says he did not "make threats" to terminate departing news boss Justin Stevens, as senators question his sudden departure.

items.title: Perth mother who lost two children to rare genetic condition wants mandatory tests
items.summary: A Perth woman who lost two children to an extremely rare medical condition calls for mandatory genetic testing for children whose cause of death is uncertain.

items.title: Hamas confirms that Israeli air strike killed its new military leader in Gaza
items.summary: An Israeli air strike in Gaza has killed new Hamas leader Mohammed Odeh, his wife and two of his children, the militant group has confirmed.

items.title: The most influential female composers in history and today
items.summary: From Hildegard von Bingen to Hildur Guðnadóttir, here are some of the most influential women of the music world in history and today.

items.title: French Open handshake drama spices up women's draw as Elena Rybakina eliminated
items.summary: There is no love lost between Tamara Korpatsch and Wang Xinyu after a testy match as Elena Rybakina's hopes of another grand slam title go up in smoke.

items.title: Wanted alleged killer Anthony Pele's social media posts 'incredibly valuable' to police
items.summary: A wanted Sydney underworld figure may have unwittingly helped police after posting social media messages mocking his underworld adversaries from overseas.

items.title: French Open: Alejandro Davidovich Fokina's coach quits via text as Novak Djokovic survives stiff test
items.summary: Alenajdro Davidovich Fokina's French Open comes to an end shortly after his coach flees to Miami after claiming he was unwell after a lunch catch-up.

items.title: Billy Slater refuses to guarantee Reece Walsh State of Origin recall despite Queensland's collapse
items.summary: Queensland's coach bats away questions about the notable absence of Brisbane's star fullback after seeing his side cough up a 20-point lead.

items.title: Five teens appear in court over alleged stolen car rampage in Cairns
items.summary: One of the teenagers charged over an alleged stolen car rampage in Cairns was on probation for similar offences at the time, a court has heard.

items.title: Richard Zoltan Babos charged with murder after alleged violent spree in Childers

items.title: Legal action filed against federal environment minister's decision to allow land clearing at NT station
items.summary: The federal environment minister's decision to wave through plans to bulldoze almost 3,000 hectares — an area 10 times the size of Sydney's CBD — of habitat, home to threatened species, will be challenged in the Federal Court.

items.title: 'Second wave' of algal bloom forecast for SA's Spencer Gulf
items.summary: Modelling predicts that the algal bloom that has plagued South Australia's coastlines since March 2025 will be moving further through the Spencer Gulf in the next few months.

items.title: Rainy weather drenching Australia's east could turn May into one of the wettest on record
items.summary: A series of low-pressure systems are drenching Australia this week, quickly turning this May into one of the wettest on record for parts of the country's east.

items.title: Stolen Generations brothers share their story ahead of Reconciliation Week
items.summary: Iain Seymour never knew he was Aboriginal until finding out he was adopted three years ago and then meeting his brother Allen Edwards, the son of a woman who played a key part in setting up a poignant memorial to the long-term effects of the Stolen Generations. Warning: This story contains images of Indigenous people who have died.

items.title: Load limits on Victorian bridges 'wreaking havoc' as councils stuggle to fund maintenance
items.summary: Councils across regional Victoria say they are being forced to ban trucks from some bridges because they cannot afford to fix them to keep them safe.

items.title: Water for 52 Olympic-sized pools lost daily in Queensland due to pipe issues, report finds
items.summary: Queensland councils are calling for more state funding to update the state's pipe network, which they say has deteriorated to a high-risk condition.

items.title: Can farmers become less reliant on synthetic fertiliser? Some are trying

items.title: Matthew Perry's assistant jailed for role in Friends actor's death

items.title: One in 10 Australian teens relying on withdrawal method and half not using condoms as STIs rise

items.title: Iran's internet is coming back online. But it's not back to normal

items.title: Australia ranks third in the world for battery capacity and WA is leading the charge

items.summary: Within the government, there's quiet confidence the battery boom will continue to gather pace and help shield Australia from both global shocks and domestic demand surges, driving prices even lower.

items.title: Israel warns all residents in southern Lebanon to leave

items.summary: Latest trusted news from the world of finance and business.

items.title: Death in custody of Patience Kuzmins leaves family heartbroken, inquest hears
items.summary: The family of a young Indigenous woman who died in a NSW jail has told an inquest that she was a talented artist who generously supported anyone in need. The inquest is examining Corrective Services NSW's search and body scan practices.

items.title: 'Optimism' as Walk for Truth ends despite no formal commitment from PM
items.summary: The Walk for Truth ended at Parliament House today and while there was not a firm commitment from the PM, many felt the response was a 'positive move'.

items.title: NT child protection bill consultation was 'limited' due to 'time constraints', department says
items.summary: Despite taking a year to prepare a controversial bill surrounding child protection, the NT Department of Children and Families says consultation with organisations was "limited".

items.title: Labor MP who unseated Peter Dutton defends electoral commission referral over empty block
items.summary: Dickson MP Ali France has been referred to the Australian Electoral Commission with claims she is registered to vote at a vacant suburban block in her north Brisbane electorate.

items.title: Albany bakery cops fines over $60,000 after inspectors find rodent faeces and animal blood on bread
items.summary: Inspectors found animal blood dripping onto bread, rodent faeces on food and spreading mould at the Albany French Hot Bread Shop, triggering prosecution and $60,000 in penalties for the owners.

items.title: Maugean skates released back into Macquarie Harbour after two years in captivity
items.summary: A pair of endangered rays taken from a Tasmanian harbour as a species "insurance policy" have been released back into the wild after helping to produce more than 400 eggs for a captive research program.

items.title: ISIS-linked woman barred from Australia tried to board flight

items.title: Drought-affected farmers welcome significant rainfall but some miss out
items.summary: Up to 100 millimetres of rain has been welcomed across drought-hit parts of western NSW, but some farmers expecting relief were left disappointed.

items.title: Billions are traded each week on Kalshi and Polymarket. There is a push to bring prediction markets to Australia
items.summary: Kalshi and Polymarket have gone from obscure start-ups to two of the world's biggest prediction markets where users bet on yes-or-no questions spanning politics, pop culture and war.

items.summary: A Midwest city council buys Crown land for $1 to develop more than 100 dwellings to house key workers, but WA's shadow local government minister says it is an example of more responsibility being placed on councils.

items.summary: Authorities keep a close eye on measles outbreaks in Asia, as many cases reported in Australia continue to be returned overseas travellers.

items.title: Adelaide track designer estimates MotoGP remodel could cost more than $100m

items.title: Cuts of meat that can save you money and how to cook with them
items.summary: If you are looking to trim your household food budget, lower-cost cuts of meat could be a good place to start. Here are some tips on what to look for and how to cook them.

items.title: Allegedly stolen cars cause peak hour traffic chaos in Cairns
items.summary: Police have arrested four teenage boys after three allegedly stolen cars caused traffic chaos across several Cairns suburbs today.

items.title: Vladimir Putin launches new incentives to recruit more soldiers for Russia's war in Ukraine
items.summary: The Kremlin is trying to recruit more soldiers for its war in Ukraine but an analyst says at the rate of casualties, "Russia cannot replace recruits fast enough to keep the 'meat grinder' fed."

items.title: BOM plans to build new radar at Tennant Creek airport, 11 years after closure
items.summary: BOM says it has decided to install a new weather radar in Tennant Creek, in the same place as the previous radar.

items.title: Since placing his first bet at age 15, Dylan says he's lost at least $900k
items.summary: From footy tips to Taylor Swift bets, teenagers are being lured into gambling. Almost 34 per cent of Australian teens gamble and everyone from teachers, experts, to the young people themselves are sounding the alarm.
